channel.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Edgar's Personal Wiki

item.title: Edgar's Personal Wiki
item.description: Edgar’s Personal Wiki Public notebook This is the public edge of a private working wiki: source-backed notes about software, systems, books, institutions, and the moments where fiction explains reality better than reality explains itself.

item.title: choosing-what-to-work-on
item.description: Choosing What to Work On Choosing what to work on is the judgment of selecting problems whose importance, evidence, timing, and fit justify sustained attention.

item.title: computational-complexity-theory
item.description: Computational Complexity Theory Computational complexity theory studies what resources are needed to solve, verify, approximate, or simulate computational problems.

item.title: quantum-computation
item.description: Quantum Computation Quantum computation is a model of computation that allows algorithms to use quantum-mechanical operations such as superposition, interference, unitary evolution, and measurement.

item.title: zero-knowledge-proofs
item.description: Zero-Knowledge Proofs Zero-knowledge proofs are interactive proofs where a prover convinces a verifier that a claim is true without revealing the underlying proof, witness, or secret beyond the truth of the claim.

item.title: avi-wigderson
item.description: Avi Wigderson Avi Wigderson is presented in the June 1, 2026 Peterman Pod episode as a theoretical computer scientist whose work spans computational complexity theory, randomness, zero-knowledge proofs, and quantum computation.

item.title: ryan-peterman
item.description: Ryan Peterman Ryan Peterman is the host of The Peterman Pod and author of The Developing Dev.

item.title: peterman-pod-avi-wigderson-complexity-theory
item.description: Peterman Pod: Avi Wigderson on P vs NP, Zero-Knowledge, and Quantum Computation This June 1, 2026 episode of The Peterman Pod features Avi Wigderson, a theoretical computer scientist discussing computational complexity theory, randomness, zero-knowledge proofs, and quantum computation.

item.title: the-peterman-pod
item.description: The Peterman Pod The Peterman Pod is a podcast hosted by Ryan Peterman.

item.title: agentic-self-service-analytics
item.description: Agentic Self-Service Analytics Agentic self-service analytics is the use of AI agents to answer business analytics questions directly for stakeholders while preserving the source-of-truth discipline that data teams normally provide.
